summary: Silent body truncation in LLM prompts (propose_edges:600 / tag_concepts:1200) degrades model judgments on 76-85% of chunks

files.note: BODY:    {body[:400]} — display-only, FINE, do not change

analysis.author: Hermes Agent
analysis.content:
Empirical chunk distribution (n=2531):
  mean=2263 chars, median=2660, p90=3534, p99=3825, max=4009.
  85% of chunks > 600 chars  (truncated by propose_edges build_pair_prompt)
  76% of chunks > 1200 chars (truncated by tag_concepts build_prompt)

The truncations are silent — the LLM receives a partial passage, makes its
PARALLELS/CONTRASTS classification or concept scores on incomplete content,
and the operator has no signal that information was withheld. propose_edges
seeing the first 600 chars of a 2660-char passage means the model judges
similarity on ~22% of the actual text — usually intro framing, not the
substantive claim being compared.

Token math under qwen 27B (32K context window):
  propose_edges full prompt: ~1,900 tokens (2 × 3K-char passages + scaffold + response) → 6% utilization
  tag_concepts full prompt:  ~1,800 tokens (1 × 3K-char + 44-concept list + response) → 5% utilization
No operational reason for the cap; the cost was paid in silently degraded
model judgments on the majority of corpus chunks.

review_tags.py:48's body[:400] is terminal-display only (the 'BODY:' line in
the CLI review row). NOT LLM-facing, leave alone.

analysis.author: Hermes Agent
analysis.content:
Two-part fix:

CORE (must ship):
  - scripts/propose_edges.py build_pair_prompt: drop both [:600] slices,
    pass full chunk_a['body'] and chunk_b['body'].
  - scripts/tag_concepts.py build_prompt: drop the [:1200] slice on
    chunk_body. Pass it through unmodified.
  The chunker (scripts/chunk.py + chunking/<tradition>.toml::max_tokens=800)
  is the single point of truth for how big a passage is. Downstream prompts
  trust the chunker. Silent caps in prompt builders are sanity guards that
  rotted into quality regressions.

OPTIONAL (configurability for future tightening):
  - Add a module-level constant or CLI flag --max-body-chars (default 0 =
    unlimited; positive = cap). Off by default. Operator can opt into a
    cap if running against a smaller-context model in the future or if a
    weird oversize chunk appears.

Regression test: a unit test on each prompt builder that constructs a
mock chunk of length 5000 chars and asserts the substring shows up in
the produced prompt verbatim — would have caught the original bug and
will catch any future regression to the slice-and-pray pattern.

Out of scope for this ticket (split off if relevant): existing staged_edges
rows produced under the [:600] regime were classified against truncated
content. They're at tier='proposed' which already hedges them. Decision on
whether to invalidate-and-re-run is operationally heavy and worth its own
ticket if pursued.
